name: Cal Collins
abstract: Cal Collins (May 5, 1933 – August 27, 2001) was an American jazz guitarist. Born in Medora, Indiana, United States, Collins first played the mandolin professionally as a bluegrass musician in the early 1950s.

interviews.title: Cal Collins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Moore
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve known Cal since I was a kid. He actually was a friend of my father’s.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Moore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Flanigan
about_quotes.evidence: a bunch of us, including Scott Hamilton, Warren Vache and I think probably Cal Collins, Connie Kay, those were the kind of people that were in the band at that time
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Flanigan interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Dave McKenna
about_quotes.evidence: plus the things I did as a sideman with Cal Collins and Scott and Warren Vache and those things in Japan and so forth.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Jon-Erik Kellso
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...That’s what Cal Collins told me this morning, he said it. [INTERVIEWEE] Well it’s true.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon-Erik Kellso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Rebecca Kilgore
about_quotes.evidence: We met about a year ago. We were both teaching at a music camp on Vashon Island in Washington State.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rebecca Kilgore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997
